title: Dynamic Rendering Need, Browser Contexts, Network Budgets, Readiness, and Cleanup · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs dynamic rendering need, browser contexts, network budgets, readiness, and cleanup from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authorized bounded Playwright render adapter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Compare server HTML, embedded data, public API, progressive enhancement, and browser output and render only when the stakeholder evidence requires it.
Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.
Route, observe, allow, block, and count document and subresource requests by type, origin, bytes, redirects, and owner policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Browser Cleanup case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 9895 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dynamic rendering need and validate isolated browser contexts, network budgets, readiness, and cleanup; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Closing the Python process is adequate browser cleanup evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close pages, contexts, browser processes, downloads, traces, temporary storage, and pending routes on success, error, timeout, and cancellation. Artifact under review: authorized bounded Playwright render adapter.

options.text: The Browser Cleanup record says authorized bounded Playwright render adapter looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Browser Cleanup.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Browser Cleanup for authorized bounded Playwright render adapter.
The record names the assumption, applies Browser Cleanup, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Browser Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Browser Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review site-quality team handles case 2514 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dynamic rendering need and validate isolated browser contexts, network budgets, readiness, and cleanup; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close pages, contexts, browser processes, downloads, traces, temporary storage, and pending routes on success, error, timeout, and cancellation.
